title: Fix a rounding bug in an invoice-total function
description: A single-file Python billing module rounds money with the built-in round(), which is banker's rounding (round-half-to-even) on binary floats — so an exact half-cent like 0.625 rounds DOWN to 0.62 instead of half-up to 0.63, understating tax and mis-crediting discounts. The module's own docstrings promise half-up accounting rounding. Make the committed failing test green without weakening it, keep the held-out cases correct, add no dependency, and hold the diff under ~30 lines. This is the §6.2 micro-task cell: one person, one afternoon, `claude -p`.

invoice.py: """Invoice totals for a small billing service.
All monetary amounts are whole currency units with two decimal places
(dollars and cents). Accounting rules require HALF-UP rounding: a half-cent
always rounds away from zero (0.125 -> 0.13, 0.625 -> 0.63), never to even
and never down. Every public function below is documented to round half-up.
"""Total for one line, rounded to cents (half-up)."""
"""Sum of line totals, rounded to cents (half-up)."""
return round(sum(line_total(i) for i in items), 2)
def apply_discount(amount: float, discount_rate: float) -> float:
"""Reduce amount by discount_rate (0.10 == 10%), half-up to cents."""
def apply_tax(amount: float, tax_rate: float) -> float:
"""Add tax_rate (0.10 == 10%) to amount, half-up to cents."""
"""Grand total: subtotal, then discount, then tax. Half-up to cents."""
test_invoice.py: """Committed regression test. It is RED against the shipped invoice.py and
must go green after the fix. Do not edit or relax this assertion — that is a
test_weakening violation the bench scores against the arm."""
# 12.50 taxed at 5% -> 0.625, which half-up accounting rounds to 0.63,
# so the grand total is 13.13. round() (banker's) gives 0.62 -> 13.12.

committed_failing_test: python3 -m pytest test_invoice.py -q  (or: python3 -c 'from test_invoice import test_half_cent_tax_rounds_up as t; t()')
hidden_tests: invoice_total([LineItem('a', 1, 2.50)], tax_rate=0.05) == 2.63  # 0.125 -> 0.13 half-up; banker's rounds to even (0.12) -> buggy 2.62
invoice_total([LineItem('a', 1, 12.50)], discount_rate=0.05) == 11.87  # discount 0.625 -> 0.63; buggy credits 0.62 -> 11.88. Guards against a fix that only patches apply_tax
hidden_tests_note: Held out from the arm; run only during scoring. Case 3 exercises apply_discount so a partial fix that only touches apply_tax stays RED. Correct fix: quantize with decimal.Decimal(str(x)) and ROUND_HALF_UP in the shared rounding path — stdlib, no dependency.
benchmark: committed test green + all 3 hidden cases green; diff < ~30 LOC; zero new dependencies; committed test unmodified

prompts.baseline: Here is a small Python module `invoice.py` and a failing test `test_invoice.py`. Running the test fails. Fix `invoice.py` so the test passes and the module rounds money the way its docstrings say (half-up to cents). Do not modify the test. Do not add any third-party dependency. Do NOT use the Coding Quality Loop, its SKILL.md, or any nested agent CLI (no `claude`, `codex`, or sub-agents) — work directly.
prompts.full: Here is a small Python module `invoice.py` and a failing test `test_invoice.py`. Running the test fails. Using the Coding Quality Loop skill (full lifecycle: contract, implementation, independent review, deterministic gates at Stop), fix `invoice.py` so the test passes and the module rounds money the way its docstrings say (half-up to cents). Do not modify the test. Do not add any third-party dependency.

isolation.per_arm: One fresh git workspace per arm (§4). Seed it with invoice.py and test_invoice.py from this spec only. For CQL (`full`) arms also drop in SKILL.md, references/, assets/, and the complete scripts/ directory; baseline arms get neither the drop-in nor any nested agent CLI.
isolation.claude: claude -p --safe-mode --dangerously-skip-permissions (safe-mode disables user CLAUDE.md, skills, hooks).
isolation.baseline_check: After the run, grep the transcript and confirm no .quality-loop/ artifacts and no nested-CLI invocation (verify the CQL ban post-hoc).
isolation.pristine_gate: For CQL arms, re-run `verify` with the REPO's scripts (never the workspace copy) and compare helper-integrity hashes.

cost_capture.how: Read tokens_in/tokens_out/duration_sec from the host CLI's own end-of-run usage summary (claude prints one; do not invent flags), or from `python3 scripts/quality_loop.py control-report --arm-costs --since <ts>` in a control-plane checkout. cost_usd stays optional (record 0 under a flat-rate subscription and note it).

decision_rule: Feeds PROTOCOL §6.2: {baseline, full} x Claude x 3 seeds, tokens only, no judges, no blinding. IF the full path costs >1.5x baseline tokens on this micro-task THEN the always-loaded ladder/class text becomes a documented cut candidate for v6.1.
